description: Ruff: An extremely fast Python linter

options.description: Enable verbose logging
Print diagnostics, but nothing else
Disable all logging (but still exit with status code "1" upon detecting diagnostics)
Path to the `pyproject.toml` or `ruff.toml` file to use for configuration
Ignore all configuration files
Print help

subcommands.description: Run Ruff on the given files or directories

options.description: Apply fixes to resolve lint violations
Include fixes that may not retain the original intent of the code
Show an enumeration of all fixed lint violations
Avoid writing any fixed files back; instead, output a diff for each changed file to stdout, and exit 0 if there are no diffs. Implies `--fix-only`
Run in watch mode by re-running whenever files change
Apply fixes to resolve lint violations, but don't report on, or exit non-zero for, leftover violations. Implies `--fix`
Ignore any `# noqa` comments
Output serialization format for violations. The default serialization format is 'full'
Specify file to write the linter output to (default: stdout)
The minimum Python version that should be supported
Enable preview mode; checks will include unstable rules and fixes
List of mappings from file extension to language (one of `python`, `ipynb`, `pyi`). For example, to treat `.ipy` files as IPython notebooks, use `--extension ipy:ipynb`
Show counts for every rule with at least one violation
Enable automatic additions of `noqa` directives to failing lines
See the files Ruff will be run against with the current settings
See the settings Ruff will use to lint a given Python file
Print help
Comma-separated list of rule codes to enable (or ALL, to enable all rules)
Comma-separated list of rule codes to disable
Like --select, but adds additional rule codes on top of the selected ones
List of mappings from file pattern to code to exclude
Like `--per-file-ignores`, but adds additional ignores on top of those already specified
List of rule codes to treat as eligible for fix. Only applicable when fix itself is enabled (e.g., via `--fix`)
List of rule codes to treat as ineligible for fix. Only applicable when fix itself is enabled (e.g., via `--fix`)
Like --fixable, but adds additional rule codes on top of those already specified
List of paths, used to omit files and/or directories from analysis
Like --exclude, but adds additional files and directories on top of those already excluded
Respect file exclusions via `.gitignore` and other standard ignore files
Enforce exclusions, even for paths passed to Ruff directly on the command-line
Disable cache reads
Path to the cache directory
The name of the file when passing it through stdin
Exit with status code "0", even upon detecting lint violations
Exit with a non-zero status code if any files were modified via fix, even if no lint violations remain

args.name: Path
args.description: The path to use for the project/script

options.description: Explain all rules
Output format [default: text]

suggestions.description: Boolean-typed positional argument in function definition
Boolean default positional argument in function definition
Boolean positional value in function call

subcommands.description: List or describe the available configuration options

options.description: Output format [default: text]
Output format

subcommands.description: Clear any caches in the current directory and any subdirectories
Run the Ruff formatter on the given files or directories

options.description: Avoid writing any formatted files back; instead, exit with a non-zero status code if any files would have been modified, and zero otherwise
Avoid writing any formatted files back; instead, exit with a non-zero status code and the difference between the current file and how the formatted file would look like
List of mappings from file extension to language (one of `python`, `ipynb`, `pyi`). For example, to treat `.ipy` files as IPython notebooks, use `--extension ipy:ipynb`
The minimum Python version that should be supported
Enable preview mode; enables unstable formatting. Use `--no-preview` to disable
Disable cache reads (env: RUFF_NO_CACHE=)
Path to the cache directory (env: RUFF_CACHE_DIR=)
The name of the file when passing it through stdin
Respect file exclusions via `.gitignore` and other standard ignore files
List of paths, used to omit files and/or directories from analysis
Enforce exclusions, even for paths passed to Ruff directly on the command-line
Set the line-length
When specified, Ruff will try to only format the code in the given range

args.name: Path
args.description: List of files or directories to format [default: .]

options.description: Enable preview mode. Use `--no-preview` to disable
Disable preview mode

subcommands.description: Run analysis over Python source code
Generate a map of Python file dependencies or dependents

options.description: The direction of the import map. By default, generates a dependency map, i.e., a map from file to files that it depends on. Use `--direction dependents` to generate a map from file to files that depend on it
Attempt to detect imports from string literals
Enable preview mode. Use `--no-preview` to disable
Disable preview mode
The minimum Python version that should be supported
